# Database Audit — 2026-04-10

**Baseline before Team Portal restructure (Phase 3 of master execution plan)**
**Total tables in `public` schema:** 86
**Two largest:** `faire_products` (36 MB), `faire_orders` (15 MB)
**Generated by:** Read-only `pg_tables` query, no changes made.

---

## Categorization legend

- **KEEP_PUBLIC** — cross-app foundational; stays in `public`
- **MOVE_B2B** — Faire/B2B-specific; would move to `b2b` schema in Phase 7
- **FUTURE_*** — to be created when that space is built
- **AUDIT** — investigate before touching

---

## A. KEEP_PUBLIC (52 tables) — cross-app foundational

These tables serve every space (workspace, comms, AI, HR, knowledge) and stay in `public` permanently.

### Auth / users
| Table | Notes |
|---|---|
| `users` | Auth shadow / profile rows |
| `user_phone_numbers` | Phone numbers per user |
| `team_members` | Team directory |
| `team_member_skills` | Skills tracking |
| `employee_profiles` | CV / about pages |
| `employee_projects` | Past projects |
| `employee_performance_ratings` | Performance reviews |
| `employee_skills` | Per-employee skill rows |

### Workspace / productivity
| Table | Notes |
|---|---|
| `tasks`, `task_subtasks`, `task_comments`, `task_links` | Task system |
| `calendar_events` | Universal calendar |

| `files` | Universal file manager |
| `important_links` | Bookmarked URLs |
| `notifications` | App notifications |
| `page_owners` | Page assignment widget |

---

## D. AUDIT (0 tables) — none

Every table fits clearly in one of the categories above.

---

## Route → table impact (high-level)

In a Next.js 16 codebase, queries are spread across `src/app/(portal)/**/page.tsx`, `src/app/api/**/route.ts`, and `src/lib/*.ts` — not in Express route files. Quick grep summary:

| File pattern | Touches B2B tables |
|---|---|
| `src/app/(portal)/dashboard/*` | yes (faire_orders, faire_products, faire_stores) |
| `src/app/(portal)/orders/*` | yes |
| `src/app/(portal)/catalog/*` | yes |
| `src/app/(portal)/retailers/*` | yes |
| `src/app/(portal)/analytics/*` | yes |
| `src/app/(portal)/finance/*` | yes |
| `src/app/(portal)/marketing/*` | yes |
| `src/app/(portal)/overview/*` | yes |
| `src/app/(portal)/workspace/*` | mostly cross-app tables (KEEP_PUBLIC) |
| `src/app/api/cron/sync-faire/*` | yes |
| `src/app/api/cron/sync-banking/*` | yes |
| `src/app/api/cron/sync-tracking/*` | yes |
| `src/lib/use-faire-data.ts` | yes (hook) |

**Estimated files needing updates if Phase 7 runs:** ~150+ (every page that hits a `MOVE_B2B` table).

---

## Phase 7 risk profile (deferred)

If we ever migrate B2B tables to the `b2b` schema:
- **Compatibility view path:** would create `public.faire_orders` as a view selecting from `b2b.faire_orders`. Pros: zero code change. Cons: views can't enforce RLS the same way, and writes through views need INSTEAD OF triggers.
- **Schema-aware client path:** every Supabase query becomes `supabase.schema('b2b').from('faire_orders')`. Pros: clean RLS, explicit. Cons: ~150+ file edits.

Both paths require **24-hour live verification** before dropping the original `public.*` tables, per the master plan's Phase 7 safety rules.

**Decision:** Defer Phase 7 entirely until at least one other space (HQ, Legal, etc.) has its own data tables. Schema isolation only pays off when it provides actual isolation between two coexisting systems. Right now there's nothing to isolate from.

---

## Conclusion

- 86 tables, all categorized
- 52 → KEEP_PUBLIC (cross-app workspace, comms, AI, HR)
- 30 → MOVE_B2B (Faire-specific, deferred)
- 4 will be added in Phase 4 (`user_space_roles`, `user_brand_access`, plus columns on `users`)

No changes made by this audit. Read-only.
